content: Migraine treatment分为 acute and preventive strategies. Acute treatment (take early in attack): (1) Mild-moderate: NSAIDs (ibuprofen, naproxen, diclofenac), acetaminophen, combination analgesics (acetaminophen/aspirin/caffeine). (2) Moderate-severe or NSAID-refractory: triptans (sumatriptan, rizatriptan, eletriptan, etc.)—5-HT1B/1D agonists causing vasoconstriction and inhibiting CGRP release; contraindicated in cardiovascular disease. Newer options: gepants (ubrogepant, rimegepant—oral CGRP antagonists, no vasoconstriction), ditans (lasmiditan—5-HT1F agonist, no vasoconstriction but causes sedation, 8-hour driving restriction). Antiemetics (metoclopramide, prochlorperazine) for nausea and as adjunct analgesics. Preventive treatment indicated for ≥4 migraine days/month, significant disability, or medication overuse: (1) Oral preventives: beta-blockers (propranolol, metoprolol), topiramate (weight loss, cognitive side effects), valproate (teratogenic), amitriptyline, venlafaxine. (2) CGRP monoclonal antibodies (erenumab, fremanezumab, galcanezumab, eptinezumab)—monthly/quarterly injections, well-tolerated, specific for migraine. (3) OnabotulinumtoxinA for chronic migraine only (PREEMPT protocol). (4) Gepants for prevention: atogepant (daily), rimegepant (every other day). Non-pharmacologic: lifestyle modification, trigger management, biofeedback, CBT, neuromodulation devices.
